title: ajuga australis var. typica
intro: Ajuga australis var. typica, also known as Bugle, is a perennial herb native to Australia, characterized by its blue-purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant contains compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Bugle have been associated with promoting digestive health, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Bugle can be prepared as a tea infusion or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
